response.justification: The candidate has a long software development career but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the absence of Japanese language proficiency — the job requires native-level Japanese, while the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and German. Beyond the language barrier, the resume shows a career focused on web, full-stack, and TypeScript/Node.js development with no meaningful exposure to embedded networking, Layer-2/3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), network switch firmware, or Linux kernel modification. C/C++ appears only in minor, dated contexts. There is no signal of fit for the core security features domain this role requires.
strengths: Long and varied software development career spanning 15+ years across multiple languages and domains, demonstrating adaptability
Some embedded and low-level systems exposure (National Instruments C/C++ work, sound synthesis with Linux/Jack/C++, hardware work at Altom)
Experience with Linux environments and DevOps tooling (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab), providing a baseline infrastructure foundation
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for the role that cannot be waived
Zero demonstrated experience with security networking technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch firmware, NETCONF, SNMP)
C programming is minimal and dated; the career is overwhelmingly web/TypeScript/Node.js focused, not systems or embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking domain experience
